original_abstract: This article features selected excerpts from Nikolai A. Voznesenskiy’s seminal work, *The Military Economy of the USSR during the Patriotic War*. As a pivotal figure in the Soviet war effort, Voznesenskiy spearheaded the development of critical military-economic strategies and oversaw the establishment of the massive industrial complex in the East. This base proved instrumental in maintaining a continuous supply of military hardware to the front lines. Beyond his strategic planning, Voznesenskiy held personal responsibility for the procurement of weaponry and ammunition for the Red Army and served as a key member of the committee tasked with reconstructing the national economy in territories liberated from Nazi occupation.
humanized_abstract: These are extracts from Nikolai A. Voznesenskiy’s book, The Military Economy of the USSR during the Patriotic War. Voznesenskiy was one of the most important Soviet leaders during the war. He played a crucial role in planning the war-time military economy and in organising the development of the East as the main industrial centre of the country, responsible for the uninterrupted provision of arms to the front. He was also in charge of the production of weapons and ammunition for the Red Army, and took part in the Council for the reconstitution of the national economy in the newly-liberated zones.

He was also in charge of the production of weapons and ammunition for the Red Army, and took part in the Council for the reconstitution of the national economy in the newly-liberated zones.
